prompt:
I inherited this branch of the yard's stock report and it won't run. The previous owner left mid-refactor, review comments say "tidy before merge", and this is what tidy looks like apparently:

    $ pwsh -NoProfile -File ./yardstock.ps1 -Path stock.csv
    ParserError: yardstock.ps1:4
    Line |
       4 |      [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Path
         |                                         ~
         | Missing ')' in function parameter list.

And it's not just the one: each time I patch whatever it's complaining about, the next run coughs up a different error, usually blaming yardstock.ps1 even when I can't see anything wrong at the line it points to. There's a lib/ folder with three dot-sourced files and I suspect the mess is spread around in there, but the errors don't say which file, which is fantastic.

The logic itself was signed off before the refactor: reorder under the threshold, watch under triple the threshold, report lines in ledger order, count summary at the bottom. Nobody is asking you to redesign anything -- just get every last error out so it runs end to end and the numbers come out right. pwsh -NoProfile -File test_yardstock.ps1 must pass; the test file is not yours to edit.
notes: 6P10 item 6 (5C-style multi-error build triage; probed on pwsh 7.6.3 2026-07-16, every dump and the full reveal chain captured by execution). 5 independent parse/binding errors across 4 files: E1 yardstock.ps1 param list missing comma (ParserError 'Missing )' -- the pasted dump); E2 lib/readers.ps1 quote juxtaposition Write-Verbose "loaded "$rows.Count" rows" -- NOT a parse error but a runtime ParameterBindingException ('A positional parameter cannot be found that accepts argument 5') that terminates under the script's EAP Stop, reported at yardstock.ps1:20, the call site; E3 lib/readers.ps1 foreach missing one closing paren next to a nested @() ('Unexpected token {', attributed to yardstock.ps1, the dot-source site, NOT the lib file); E4 lib/rules.ps1 'else if' instead of 'elseif' in an if-expression assignment ('Missing statement block after else keyword', again blamed on yardstock.ps1); E5 lib/render.ps1 $Entry vs $Entries typo in the summary line -- strict-mode 'variable cannot be retrieved' AFTER all five report lines print, attributed to the Format-Stock call in main. Chain-reveal (documented per 5C law): dot-sourced files parse at dot-source time, so E3/E4 surface one file per run after E1; E2 surfaces only once readers PARSES (its binding error is runtime); E5 is last, visible only when everything else runs. Every single error is reported far from its cause -- pwsh attributes lib parse errors to the dot-source line in main, which the prompt's user calls out without understanding. Reference fix: comma, paren, string concat, elseif, $Entries -- five one-line edits, logic untouched, behavior pinned byte-exact for two thresholds plus typed-binding rejection and missing-file 66. Differentiation vs wave-5 siblings c-gritroute/cpp-cellarlog (5C4 multi-error compile triage): PowerShell-native pin is that there is no compiler pass over the tree -- errors materialize one dot-sourced FILE at a time at runtime and are attributed to the includer, an error-topology C/C++ compilers (which list all files' diagnostics up front) never exhibit. Verify run 5x during self-check, deterministic.
